skill.name: frontend-ui-dark-ts
skill.description: Build dark-themed React applications with Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, Framer Motion, reusable UI components, and responsive dashboard patterns.
skill.summary: Create polished dark React dashboards with Tailwind theming and motion patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are markdown examples, placeholder metadata, or normal project-relative asset paths. The only real caution is that the quick start asks users to run npm and npx package manager commands, which is expected for a React setup guide but should be user-controlled. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential handling, data exfiltration, or hidden execution logic.

low_findings.title: Package Manager Commands in Setup Guide
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE, low severity. The quick start includes npm and npx commands that install packages and initialize Tailwind. This is expected for a frontend setup guide, but users should review package sources before running commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present and would execute external package manager tooling if copied. The surrounding context is a standard React project setup guide, not hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Alert Is a Type Annotation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported import expression is inside a Tailwind JSDoc type comment and is not runtime dynamic code loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a JSDoc comment declaring a Tailwind config type. No executable dynamic import or user-controlled module path is present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder External URLs in Metadata Example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to example.com inside sample Open Graph and Twitter metadata, with no fetch or outbound request logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as placeholder HTML meta tag values. There is no credential handling, webhook target, or network API call.

low_findings.title: Relative Font Paths Are Not Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The ../ sequences are CSS references from styles to local font assets in the documented project structure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are static CSS font URLs and match the local asset layout shown earlier in the file. They do not consume user input or access sensitive locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography hits correspond to normal descriptive text, HTML metadata labels, and .md reference links, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain descriptions, metadata fields, or markdown link filenames. No hash function, cipher, password storage, or crypto API usage is present.

content.value_statement: Dark application interfaces often become inconsistent when teams mix colors, spacing, and animation patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concrete React and Tailwind blueprint for cohesive dashboards.
actual_capabilities: Provides a React, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion starter structure.
Defines dark theme colors, typography, spacing, shadows, and status colors.
Shows reusable UI component folders for buttons, cards, inputs, badges, dialogs, and tabs.
Includes CSS utilities for glass cards, overlays, inputs, panels, focus states, and scrollbars.
Documents Framer Motion patterns for fades, slides, hover scale, page transitions, and staggered children.
Includes mobile viewport, safe area, touch target, favicon, social metadata, and PWA manifest examples.
limitations: It is a design and implementation guide, not a complete generated application.
It assumes React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, and Framer Motion are acceptable dependencies.
It does not include backend integration, authentication, data fetching, or state management architecture.
Referenced extended files under references are listed but not present in the scanned package.

use_cases.title: Create an Admin Dashboard Shell
use_cases.description: Generate a dark React app shell with routing, sidebar layout, page headers, and consistent navigation surfaces.

use_cases.title: Standardize Product UI Tokens
use_cases.description: Apply shared colors, spacing, typography, borders, focus states, and status colors across a TypeScript interface.

use_cases.title: Prototype Animated Data Views
use_cases.description: Build polished cards, panels, modals, and page transitions for demos without inventing motion patterns from scratch.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Dark React App
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a Vite React TypeScript app with Tailwind CSS, dark theme tokens, and the documented folder structure.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Dashboard Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build a dashboard shell with a glass sidebar, page header, routing, responsive spacing, and accessible focus states.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Existing Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to refactor my existing React components into the documented dark theme, typography, color, and glass utility patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Animated Admin Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an admin workflow with tabs, dialogs, cards, staggered lists, page transitions, and consistent status color usage.

output_examples.input: Create a dark analytics dashboard shell.
output_examples.output: A React TypeScript layout with an app shell, sidebar, dashboard route, glass panels, Tailwind theme tokens, and subtle page transitions.

output_examples.input: Make my settings page match a dark design system.
output_examples.output: A settings interface using shared text styles, neutral backgrounds, brand actions, accessible focus rings, and reusable card and input patterns.

output_examples.input: Add motion to dashboard cards and modals.
output_examples.output: A motion plan using fade, slide, scale, overlay, modal, and stagger patterns with short durations and consistent easing.

best_practices: Review generated npm and npx commands before running them in your project.
Keep theme tokens centralized in Tailwind config and global CSS variables.
Use animations to clarify state changes, not to distract from dense information.
anti_patterns: Do not mix unrelated color palettes with the documented dark theme tokens.
Do not apply glass effects to every surface when plain contrast is clearer.
Do not copy placeholder metadata URLs into production without replacing them.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete application?
faq.answer: No. It provides a strong frontend structure, theme system, and implementation patterns for React applications.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: It targets React with TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS, React Router, and Framer Motion.

faq.question: Can I use it without Framer Motion?
faq.answer: Yes, but the documented animation examples assume Framer Motion and should be adapted or removed.

faq.question: Is the dark theme customizable?
faq.answer: Yes. The Tailwind config and CSS variables define colors, spacing, radius, shadows, and status colors.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: It includes focus styles, touch target sizing, safe area spacing, and readable text color patterns.

faq.question: Are there security concerns?
faq.answer: The main caution is standard package manager setup commands. Review dependencies before running install commands.
